items.message: Removed comment prop from FormWrapperProps and FormWrapper, but the prop was previously used for fallback name/expertise. This is a breaking change for any other component that passed comment to FormWrapper.
items.suggestion: Ensure no other components pass the comment prop to FormWrapper, or add a deprecation warning if backward compatibility is needed.

items.message: Removed comment={parent} prop from FormWrapper in reply-form.tsx, which is consistent with the removal of the comment prop from FormWrapper. However, this means the reply form no longer has access to the parent comment's member info for fallback.
items.suggestion: Confirm that the reply form's member context (from useAppContext) always provides the correct logged-in member, and that no fallback to parent comment author is needed.

items.message: Editor editability now depends on member.expertise instead of memberName, which may prevent editing when the member has a name but no expertise.
items.suggestion: Consider using memberName (or member?.name) as the condition for editability, or add a separate check for expertise if that is the intended behavior.

items.message: Removed fallback to comment?.member?.name and comment?.member?.expertise in FormWrapper; reply forms will no longer inherit parent comment author's name/expertise, which is the intended fix but may break existing behavior if any other caller relied on the fallback.
items.suggestion: Verify that all callers of FormWrapper (including reply-form.tsx) pass the correct member context and that no other component depends on the old fallback behavior.

items.message: Changed in_reply_to_id from openForm.in_reply_to_id to parent.id. This may change the reply threading behavior if openForm.in_reply_to_id differed from parent.id (e.g., when replying to a nested reply).
items.suggestion: Verify that parent.id is the correct identifier for the reply target in all cases, especially when replying to nested replies.

review_guidelines.title: Migration Files Must Follow Strict Naming Convention
review_guidelines.objective: Ensure database migration files follow the required timestamp-based naming pattern to maintain chronological order and enable proper migration tracking
review_guidelines.success_criteria: Migration files in core/server/data/migrations/versions/ (excluding versions 1.x, 2.x, 3.x) match the pattern: YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM-descriptive-name-with-hyphens (e.g., 2022-04-05-12-00-add-member-alert-setting.js)
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Migration files do not follow the required timestamp format or use underscores, camelCase, or other naming styles

review_guidelines.title: Enforce Module Boundary Between core/shared and core/server
review_guidelines.objective: Maintain architectural separation by preventing core/shared modules from depending on core/server code, ensuring shared code remains truly reusable across server and frontend
review_guidelines.success_criteria: Files in core/shared/ do not contain require() or import statements that reference core/server/ paths
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Files in core/shared/ import or require modules from core/server/

review_guidelines.title: Enforce Module Boundary Between core/shared and core/frontend
review_guidelines.objective: Maintain architectural separation by preventing core/shared modules from depending on core/frontend code, ensuring shared code remains truly reusable across server and frontend
review_guidelines.success_criteria: Files in core/shared/ do not contain require() or import statements that reference core/frontend/ paths
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Files in core/shared/ import or require modules from core/frontend/

review_guidelines.title: Database Schema Must Not Include User Audit Fields
review_guidelines.objective: Prevent using created_by and updated_by fields in database schema to enforce the use of the action log for tracking user actions, providing better audit trails and separation of concerns
review_guidelines.success_criteria: The schema definition in core/server/data/schema/schema.js does not contain properties named 'created_by' or 'updated_by'
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: The schema definition includes 'created_by' or 'updated_by' fields

review_guidelines.title: Database Migrations Must Not Use Loop Constructs
review_guidelines.objective: Prevent performance issues in database migrations by prohibiting loop constructs (for, while, forEach) that can cause migrations to run slowly or fail on large datasets
review_guidelines.success_criteria: Migration files in core/server/data/migrations/versions/ do not contain for statements, for-of statements, for-in statements, while statements, forEach calls, or _.each calls
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Migration files contain any loop constructs including for, for-of, for-in, while, forEach, or _.each

review_guidelines.title: Database Migrations Must Not Use Multiple JOIN Statements
review_guidelines.objective: Prevent complex and poorly performing database queries in migrations by prohibiting multiple JOIN operations in a single Knex query block
review_guidelines.success_criteria: Migration files do not chain multiple join/innerJoin/leftJoin calls on a single Knex query object
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Migration files contain Knex queries with multiple chained join operations

review_guidelines.title: Database Migrations Must Not Return Early from Loops
review_guidelines.objective: Prevent data processing errors in migrations by ensuring all records are processed when loops are used (in legacy migrations or utility functions)
review_guidelines.success_criteria: Migration files do not contain return statements inside loop bodies
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Migration files contain return statements within loop constructs

review_guidelines.title: API Endpoint Controllers Must Have Type Annotations
review_guidelines.objective: Ensure type safety and IDE support for API endpoint controllers by requiring JSDoc type annotations from @tryghost/api-framework
review_guidelines.success_criteria: API endpoint controller objects in core/server/api/endpoints/ include JSDoc type comment '@type {import('@tryghost/api-framework').Controller}'
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: API endpoint controllers lack the required JSDoc type annotation

review_guidelines.title: API Endpoints Must Enforce Complexity Limits
review_guidelines.objective: Maintain code quality and prevent overly complex API endpoint implementations by enforcing complexity metrics on endpoint handler functions
review_guidelines.success_criteria: API endpoint files in core/server/api/endpoints/ pass the 'ghost/ghost-custom/max-api-complexity' ESLint rule
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: API endpoint functions exceed the maximum allowed cyclomatic complexity threshold

review_guidelines.title: Internationalization Files Must Use Kebab-Case Naming
review_guidelines.objective: Ensure consistent file naming across all i18n translation files to maintain a uniform codebase structure and simplify automated tooling
review_guidelines.success_criteria: All files in ghost/i18n/ use kebab-case naming (lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, and dots only): matching pattern ^[a-z0-9.-]+$
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: i18n files use camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, or contain uppercase letters

review_guidelines.title: Admin React Apps Must Use Kebab-Case File Naming
review_guidelines.objective: Enforce consistent kebab-case file naming convention across React admin applications to align with the codebase migration to kebab-case naming standards
review_guidelines.success_criteria: All files in apps/admin-x-* directories use kebab-case naming (lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, and dots only): matching pattern ^[a-z0-9.-]+$
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Admin app files use camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, or contain uppercase letters

review_guidelines.title: React Components Must Sort JSX Props in Standard Order
review_guidelines.objective: Maintain consistent and readable JSX code by enforcing a standard ordering of component props: reserved props first, callbacks last, shorthand props last
review_guidelines.success_criteria: JSX elements in React files have props ordered according to: reserved props (key, ref) first, then regular props alphabetically, then callbacks, then shorthand props last
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: JSX elements have props in non-standard order (e.g., callbacks before regular props, shorthand before callbacks)

review_guidelines.title: React Button Elements Must Have Explicit Type Attribute
review_guidelines.objective: Prevent unintended form submissions and unclear button semantics by requiring all button elements to explicitly specify their type attribute (button, submit, or reset)
review_guidelines.success_criteria: All <button> elements in React files include an explicit type attribute
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Button elements lack a type attribute or rely on browser default behavior

review_guidelines.title: React List Rendering Must Not Use Array Index as Key
review_guidelines.objective: Prevent rendering bugs and performance issues by prohibiting the use of array indices as React key props, which can cause incorrect component state when lists are reordered or modified
review_guidelines.success_criteria: React components rendering lists use stable, unique identifiers (e.g., item.id) as key props, not array indices
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: List items use array index as the key prop (e.g., key={index} or key={i})

review_guidelines.title: Tailwind CSS Classes Must Follow Standard Ordering
review_guidelines.objective: Ensure consistent and maintainable Tailwind CSS class lists by enforcing the standard ordering convention defined in the Tailwind configuration
review_guidelines.success_criteria: Tailwind utility classes in className attributes follow the ordering specified in tailwind.config.cjs (layout, positioning, sizing, spacing, typography, visual, etc.)
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Tailwind classes are in non-standard order or randomly organized

review_guidelines.title: TypeScript Files Must Enable Strict Type Checking
review_guidelines.objective: Ensure type safety and catch potential runtime errors at compile time by requiring strict mode and noImplicitAny in all TypeScript configurations
review_guidelines.success_criteria: tsconfig.json files include 'strict': true and 'noImplicitAny': true in compilerOptions
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: TypeScript configuration files have strict or noImplicitAny set to false or omitted

review_guidelines.title: Code Must Use 4-Space Indentation
review_guidelines.objective: Maintain consistent code formatting across the codebase by enforcing 4-space indentation for all source files (except JSON and YAML which use 2 spaces)
review_guidelines.success_criteria: All JavaScript, TypeScript, and other source files use 4 spaces for indentation levels
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Source files use tabs, 2-space indentation, or mixed indentation styles

review_guidelines.title: Code Must Use Single Quotes for Strings
review_guidelines.objective: Ensure consistent string literal formatting across the codebase by requiring single quotes for all string values (template literals are allowed)
review_guidelines.success_criteria: String literals in JavaScript and TypeScript files use single quotes, except when using template literals for interpolation
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: String literals use double quotes without justified reason

review_guidelines.title: Code Must Always Use Semicolons
review_guidelines.objective: Prevent subtle bugs from automatic semicolon insertion and maintain explicit statement termination by requiring semicolons at the end of all statements
review_guidelines.success_criteria: All JavaScript and TypeScript statements end with explicit semicolons
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Statements rely on automatic semicolon insertion (ASI) instead of explicit semicolons

review_guidelines.title: Code Must Use let or const Instead of var
review_guidelines.objective: Prevent hoisting-related bugs and unclear variable scoping by prohibiting var declarations in favor of block-scoped let and const
review_guidelines.success_criteria: All variable declarations use let (for reassignable variables) or const (for constants), never var
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Code contains var declarations

review_guidelines.title: Code Must Use Strict Equality Operators
review_guidelines.objective: Prevent type coercion bugs by requiring strict equality checks (=== and !==) instead of loose equality (== and !=)
review_guidelines.success_criteria: All equality comparisons use === or !== operators
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Code contains == or != operators for equality checks

review_guidelines.title: Code Must Not Use console.* Calls
review_guidelines.objective: Prevent debug statements from being committed to production code by prohibiting console.log, console.warn, and other console method calls
review_guidelines.success_criteria: Source files do not contain console.log, console.warn, console.error, or other console method calls
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Code contains calls to console methods

review_guidelines.title: Package Manager Must Be Yarn v1
review_guidelines.objective: Ensure consistent dependency management and workspace functionality by requiring Yarn v1 for all package operations, as the monorepo relies on Yarn workspaces
review_guidelines.success_criteria: All package management commands (install, add, remove, run) use yarn, not npm or other package managers
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Code documentation, scripts, or CI configuration references npm or other package managers for dependency management

review_guidelines.title: Node.js Version Must Match Engine Requirement
review_guidelines.objective: Ensure compatibility and prevent runtime errors by enforcing the specific Node.js version required by the Ghost application
review_guidelines.success_criteria: Development and production environments use Node.js version matching the engines.node specification in ghost/core/package.json (^22.13.1)
review_guidelines.failure_criteria: Environment uses a Node.js version outside the specified semver range

needs_human_review.message: Removed comment prop from FormWrapperProps and FormWrapper, but the prop was previously used for fallback name/expertise. This is a breaking change for any other component that passed comment to FormWrapper.
needs_human_review.suggestion: Ensure no other components pass the comment prop to FormWrapper, or add a deprecation warning if backward compatibility is needed.

needs_human_review.message: Removed comment={parent} prop from FormWrapper in reply-form.tsx, which is consistent with the removal of the comment prop from FormWrapper. However, this means the reply form no longer has access to the parent comment's member info for fallback.
needs_human_review.suggestion: Confirm that the reply form's member context (from useAppContext) always provides the correct logged-in member, and that no fallback to parent comment author is needed.

discarded.message: Editor editability now depends on member.expertise instead of memberName, which may prevent editing when the member has a name but no expertise.
discarded.suggestion: Consider using memberName (or member?.name) as the condition for editability, or add a separate check for expertise if that is the intended behavior.

discarded.message: Removed fallback to comment?.member?.name and comment?.member?.expertise in FormWrapper; reply forms will no longer inherit parent comment author's name/expertise, which is the intended fix but may break existing behavior if any other caller relied on the fallback.
discarded.suggestion: Verify that all callers of FormWrapper (including reply-form.tsx) pass the correct member context and that no other component depends on the old fallback behavior.

discarded.message: Changed in_reply_to_id from openForm.in_reply_to_id to parent.id. This may change the reply threading behavior if openForm.in_reply_to_id differed from parent.id (e.g., when replying to a nested reply).
discarded.suggestion: Verify that parent.id is the correct identifier for the reply target in all cases, especially when replying to nested replies.

lines.content: // Disable editing if the member doesn't have a name or when we are submitting the form
openStyles = isReplyToReply ? 'pl-[1px] pt-[68px] sm:pl-[44px] sm:pt-[56px]' : 'pl-[1px] pt-[48px] sm:pl-[44px] sm:pt-[40px]';
test('Reply form does not inherit expertise from parent comment author', async ({page}) => {
// Set logged-in member with NO expertise
// Add a comment from a different member who HAS expertise

html: '

This is a comment from someone with expertise

',
member: buildMember({name: 'Expert User', expertise: 'Head of Comments'})
// Verify the parent comment shows the author's expertise
// Click reply on the parent comment
// Wait for reply form to appear
// The reply form should show the logged-in member's info, NOT the parent's
// The expertise button should show "Add your expertise" since the
// logged-in member has no expertise - it should NOT show "Head of Comments"
test('Reply form does not inherit name from parent comment author', async ({page}) => {
// Set logged-in member with NO name (null triggers the fallback bug)
// Add a comment from a different member who HAS a name

html: '

This is a comment from someone with a name

',
// Verify the parent comment shows the author's name
// Click reply on the parent comment
// Wait for reply form to appear
// The reply form should NOT show the parent comment author's name
// It should either show nothing, "Anonymous", or prompt for a name

diff:apps/comments-ui/src/components/con.message: Removed line: const memberName = member?.name ?? comment?.member?.name;
Removed line: const memberExpertise = member?.expertise ?? comment?.member?.expertise;
Added line: <FormWrapper editor={editor} isOpen={true} openForm={openForm} reduced={isMobile()}>

',

Added line: // logged-in member has no expertise - it should NOT show "Head of Comments"

This is a comment from someone with a name

',
